articles.headline: Supreme Court Expands Scope of 'Polluter Pays' Principle, Mandates Advanced Environmental Restoration Funds
articles.explainer:
• The Apex Court upholds and significantly expands the 'Polluter Pays' principle, emphasizing proactive measures.
• Directs industrial units in ecologically sensitive zones to contribute to a central environmental restoration corpus.
• Mandates a national framework for robust environmental damage assessment, compensation, and restoration disbursement.
• The ruling aims to shift focus from punitive actions to comprehensive, long-term ecological sustainability.
• Impacts industries, environmental governance, and future infrastructure projects across the country.
gsSubTopics: Environmental pollution & degradation
environmental impact assessment
conservation
government policies & interventions
judiciary
sustainable development
prelimsPoints: Date of the Supreme Court ruling: 24 October 2025.
Key aspects: proactive environmental restoration funds, central corpus, national framework for damage assessment.
Principle first recognized in India in Vellore Citizens' Welfare Forum v. Union of India (1996).
Constitutional articles related to environment: Art 21 (right to life), Art 48A (protection & improvement of environment), Art 51A(g) (fundamental duty to protect environment).
Role of NGT (National Green Tribunal) in environmental justice.
mainsPoints: Significance of the ruling for environmental jurisprudence in India and its potential to strengthen environmental protection.
Challenges in implementation: accurate damage assessment, efficient fund collection and disbursement, monitoring restoration projects.
Impact on industrial development: balancing economic growth with environmental sustainability, potential for increased compliance costs.
Role of the judiciary in environmental governance and filling policy gaps.
Comparison with international environmental compensation mechanisms and best practices.
Discuss the 'Public Trust Doctrine' in the context of this judgment.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a green landscape with an industrial complex in the background, symbolizing environmental impact and restoration efforts.
articles.trivia: The 'Polluter Pays' Principle was incorporated into Principle 16 of the Rio Declaration on Environment and Development (1992).

articles.headline: India's Q2 FY26 GDP Growth Projected to Remain Robust Amid Global Headwinds
articles.explainer:
• Leading economic indicators suggest India's GDP growth for Q2 FY26 (July-September 2025) will maintain a strong trajectory.
• Manufacturing and services sectors are key drivers, showing resilience despite global economic uncertainties.
• Government capital expenditure and private consumption continue to provide significant impetus to growth.
• Inflation remains a monitored factor, with RBI maintaining a cautious stance on monetary policy.
• Analysts point to structural reforms and infrastructure push as foundational strengths.

articles.headline: India Hosts 15th 'Delhi Dialogue' Focusing on Strengthening ASEAN-India Strategic Partnership
articles.explainer:
• The 15th edition of the annual Delhi Dialogue is underway, bringing together policymakers, academics, and business leaders from India and ASEAN nations.
• The theme for this year emphasizes enhancing economic, strategic, and cultural cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region.
• Discussions focus on maritime security, digital economy integration, sustainable development, and regional connectivity.
• India reiterates its 'Act East Policy' commitment and its central role in fostering regional stability and prosperity.
• The dialogue aims to identify new avenues for collaboration and address common challenges.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests

mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the Delhi Dialogue in enhancing India's engagement with ASEAN and its 'Act East Policy'.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India-ASEAN cooperation in the evolving Indo-Pacific geopolitical landscape.
Evaluate the potential for deeper economic integration, including India's stance on RCEP and other trade agreements.
Examine the role of India and ASEAN in maintaining regional stability and addressing maritime security concerns.
Suggest measures to further strengthen people-to-people and cultural ties between India and Southeast Asian nations.
articles.imageDescription: Logo of the Delhi Dialogue with flags of India and ASEAN member states in the background.
articles.trivia: India became a Sectoral Dialogue Partner of ASEAN in 1992 and a full Dialogue Partner in 1995.

articles.headline: ISRO Announces Successful Orbital Maneuver for Venus Orbiter Mission 'Shukrayaan-1'
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) confirms the successful completion of a crucial orbital maneuver for its Venus Orbiter Mission, Shukrayaan-1.
• This maneuver propels the spacecraft into its final scientific observation orbit around Venus.
• Shukrayaan-1 is designed to study Venus's atmospheric composition, surface features, and subsurface structure.
• The mission carries several indigenous scientific payloads, including a high-resolution synthetic aperture radar (SAR).
• This marks a significant milestone in India's interplanetary exploration capabilities.

Achievements of Indians in science & technology

Space

Target Planet: Venus.
Primary objectives: study atmosphere, surface, subsurface of Venus.
Key payload example: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR).
India's past interplanetary missions: Chandrayaan series (Moon), Mars Orbiter Mission (Mangalyaan).
mainsPoints: Discuss the scientific objectives and potential discoveries of ISRO's Shukrayaan-1 mission for planetary science.
Analyze the significance of this mission for India's space program and its growing capabilities in interplanetary exploration.
Evaluate the role of indigenous technology in the success of such complex missions and its implications for 'Atmanirbhar Bharat'.
Compare Shukrayaan-1 with other international missions to Venus and its unique contributions.
Examine the challenges involved in designing and executing missions to Venus, given its extreme environment.
articles.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of the Shukrayaan-1 spacecraft orbiting Venus, with its scientific instruments highlighted.
articles.trivia: Venus is often called Earth's 'sister planet' due to similar size and mass, but has a vastly different, extremely hot and toxic atmosphere.

articles.headline: Union Cabinet Approves 'National Urban Green Mobility Policy 2025'
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet has given its nod to the 'National Urban Green Mobility Policy 2025', aimed at fostering sustainable urban transport.
• The policy focuses on promoting electric vehicles (EVs), public transport, non-motorized transport, and smart mobility solutions.
• Key objectives include reducing carbon emissions, improving air quality, and enhancing urban liveability.
• It mandates states and urban local bodies to develop city-specific Green Mobility Plans (GMPs) with central support.
• The policy integrates technological innovations and financial incentives for adoption of green mobility.

mainsPoints: Analyze the potential impact of the National Urban Green Mobility Policy 2025 on India's efforts to achieve its climate targets and improve urban air quality.
Discuss the challenges in implementing such a comprehensive policy, considering diverse urban landscapes and infrastructure gaps.
Evaluate the role of different stakeholders (central government, state governments, ULBs, private sector, citizens) in ensuring the policy's success.

Suggest innovative financial models and technological interventions to accelerate the adoption of green mobility solutions.
articles.imageDescription: Digital illustration of a modern city skyline with electric buses, bicycles, and green infrastructure, representing sustainable urban mobility.
articles.trivia: India is a signatory to the Paris Agreement, committing to reduce its emissions intensity by 45% by 2030 from 2005 levels.

articles.headline: India-Japan Joint Military Exercise 'Dharma Guardian' Concludes, Focus on Counter-Terrorism
articles.explainer:
• The annual joint military exercise 'Dharma Guardian' between India and Japan has successfully concluded in Rajasthan.
• The exercise focused on enhancing interoperability, sharing best practices, and developing joint strategies for counter-terrorism operations.
• Troops from both nations engaged in urban warfare scenarios, special heliborne operations, and precision target engagement drills.
• Emphasizes the growing strategic partnership between India and Japan in regional security.
• The exercise contributes to strengthening defense cooperation and mutual understanding.

Evaluate the role of India-Japan partnership in maintaining regional stability and a free and open Indo-Pacific.

articles.imageDescription: Soldiers from Indian and Japanese armies conducting a joint drill in a desert environment, symbolizing cooperation.

articles.headline: New Report Highlights Increasing Frequency of Extreme Weather Events in Indian Subcontinent
articles.explainer:
• A comprehensive report by the Indian Meteorological Department (IMD) and a consortium of environmental agencies warns of a significant rise in extreme weather events.
• The report details increased frequency and intensity of heatwaves, droughts, floods, and cyclones across the Indian subcontinent.
• Attributes the trend primarily to anthropogenic climate change and regional atmospheric-oceanic interactions.
• Calls for urgent adaptation strategies, improved early warning systems, and climate-resilient infrastructure.
• Emphasizes the disproportionate impact on vulnerable communities and agricultural sectors.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Report released by IMD and environmental agencies.
Types of events: heatwaves, droughts, floods, cyclones.

Solutions highlighted: adaptation, early warning systems, resilient infrastructure.
Vulnerable sectors: agriculture, coastal communities.
India's climate action plans: National Action Plan on Climate Change (NAPCC).
mainsPoints: Analyze the findings of the report regarding the increasing frequency of extreme weather events in India and their socio-economic implications.
Discuss the challenges faced by India in building climate resilience and implementing effective disaster management strategies.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current early warning systems and suggest improvements for better preparedness and response.
Examine the disproportionate impact of climate change on vulnerable populations and agricultural livelihoods in India.
Propose policy measures and technological interventions for robust climate adaptation and mitigation strategies, aligning with India's international commitments.
articles.imageDescription: Montage of images showing different extreme weather events: a flooded street, a parched field, and a swirling cyclone, symbolizing climate impact.
articles.trivia: The Indian subcontinent is particularly vulnerable to climate change due to its long coastline, diverse topography, and high population density.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Bharat-e-Mandi' Platform to Boost Agricultural Trade for FPOs
articles.explainer:
• The Union Agriculture Ministry has launched 'Bharat-e-Mandi', a national e-commerce platform specifically for agricultural produce.
• The platform aims to connect Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs) directly with buyers, reducing intermediaries and ensuring better prices for farmers.
• It integrates warehousing, logistics, and quality assurance services to streamline the agricultural supply chain.
• The initiative is part of the government's broader vision to double farmers' income and empower FPOs.
• Offers a transparent and efficient marketplace, leveraging digital technology for agricultural trade.

articles.trivia: The concept of FPOs was promoted to help small and marginal farmers collectivize, gain economies of scale, and improve their bargaining power.

articles.headline: National Education Policy 2020: Focus on Vocational Training Integration in Higher Education
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education highlights significant progress in integrating vocational training into mainstream higher education as per NEP 2020.
• Universities and colleges are introducing skill-based courses, apprenticeships, and industry collaborations to enhance employability.
• The policy aims to break down rigid boundaries between academic and vocational streams, offering flexible learning pathways.
• Emphasizes experiential learning and credit transfer mechanisms for vocational courses.
• This move is crucial for addressing the skill gap in the workforce and promoting holistic education.

articles.headline: India Calls for Urgent UN Security Council Reforms at UNGA Session
articles.explainer:
• India's representative at the United Nations General Assembly (UNGA) reiterated the urgent need for comprehensive reforms of the UN Security Council (UNSC).
• Emphasized that the current structure of the UNSC does not reflect contemporary global realities and power dynamics.
• Called for an expansion of both permanent and non-permanent seats, with India advocating for its rightful place as a permanent member.
• Highlighted the necessity for a more representative, transparent, and effective global governance body.
• The demand aligns with the long-standing G4 group's push for UNSC expansion.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
prelimsPoints: UNSC has 5 permanent members (P5): USA, UK, France, Russia, China.
UNSC has 10 non-permanent members elected for two-year terms.
India is a part of the G4 group (India, Brazil, Germany, Japan) advocating for permanent UNSC seats.
UNGA is the main deliberative, policymaking, and representative organ of the UN.
The need for UNSC reform is based on changed geopolitical realities since 1945.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's persistent demand for a permanent seat in the UNSC and the justifications for its claim.
Discuss the structural and political challenges hindering UNSC reforms, including resistance from existing P5 members.
Evaluate the implications of an unreformed UNSC for global peace, security, and the legitimacy of multilateralism.
Examine the arguments for and against the expansion of both permanent and non-permanent categories of membership.
Suggest a roadmap for India and like-minded nations to build consensus and advance the agenda for comprehensive UN reforms.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting the UN Security Council chamber with India's flag prominently displayed among other nations, symbolizing its aspiration for a permanent seat.
articles.trivia: The last major reform of the UNSC occurred in 1965, when the number of non-permanent members was increased from six to ten.

articles.headline: Breakthrough in Indigenous Development of Next-Gen Quantum Computing Components
articles.explainer:
• Indian scientists at the National Quantum Mission (NQM) have announced a significant breakthrough in developing indigenous components for quantum computers.
• The research focuses on stable qubit architectures and advanced cryogenic cooling systems, crucial for quantum supremacy.
• This development places India among a select few nations working on full-stack quantum technology.
• Aims to reduce reliance on foreign technology and accelerate India's quantum computing capabilities for strategic applications.
• The NQM is a government initiative to foster research and development in quantum technologies.

Indigenization of technology and developing new technology

articles.imageDescription: Abstract digital illustration of a quantum processor chip with glowing qubits, symbolizing advanced technology.
articles.trivia: Unlike classical bits (0 or 1), qubits can exist in a superposition of states (both 0 and 1 simultaneously), allowing for exponentially more complex calculations.

articles.headline: Centre-State Financial Relations: 16th Finance Commission Begins Consultations with States
articles.explainer:
• The 16th Finance Commission has commenced its consultative meetings with state governments and other stakeholders.
• The commission's primary mandate is to recommend the vertical and horizontal devolution of taxes between the Centre and states.
• Discussions focus on states' fiscal positions, revenue mobilization challenges, and expenditure requirements.
• Also tasked with reviewing disaster relief funds and suggesting measures for fiscal consolidation.
• Recommendations are crucial for shaping India's federal fiscal architecture for the next five years.

articles.headline: Ministry of Jal Shakti Launches 'Jal Jeevan Mission 2.0' for Sustainable Water Management
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti has unveiled 'Jal Jeevan Mission 2.0', building on the success of the initial phase, with an expanded focus on sustainable water management.
• The new phase emphasizes source sustainability, water quality monitoring, greywater management, and water conservation at the community level.
• Aims to ensure 'Har Ghar Jal' (tap water to every rural household) with a greater thrust on long-term operational and maintenance mechanisms.
• Integrates advanced technology for real-time monitoring and citizen engagement for water governance.
• Addresses challenges like water scarcity, contamination, and equitable distribution in rural areas.

articles.headline: India's Manufacturing Sector Sees Significant FDI Inflow in Q2 2025

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Discovers Ancient Trade Route Artifacts in Ladakh
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has unearthed a significant collection of artifacts along what is believed to be an ancient trade route in Ladakh.
• Discoveries include pottery shards, ancient coins, tools, and remnants of temporary settlements, dating back to the Kushan period.
• The findings provide crucial insights into historical trade networks connecting India with Central Asia and Tibet.
• Highlights Ladakh's strategic importance as a cultural and economic crossroads in ancient times.
• Further excavation is underway to map the full extent of the trade route and its associated sites.

articles.headline: Coastal Security Enhanced with New Surveillance Technologies

articles.headline: Government Launches 'National Cyber Security Strategy 2025' to Fortify Digital Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has launched the 'National Cyber Security Strategy 2025'.
• The strategy aims to create a robust, resilient, and secure cyberspace for all citizens and critical infrastructure.
• Focuses on strengthening cyber defense capabilities, promoting cyber education and skill development, and fostering international cooperation.
• Includes measures for data protection, incident response mechanisms, and regulatory frameworks for emerging technologies.
• This comprehensive policy is crucial for safeguarding India's digital economy and national security.

money-laundering and its prevention

role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges

articles.headline: India's Ranking in Global Hunger Index 2025 Under Scrutiny; Government Cites Methodological Flaws
articles.explainer:
• The latest Global Hunger Index (GHI) 2025 report places India at a concerning rank, prompting renewed debate on food security.
• The government has again raised objections, citing methodological flaws and lack of ground reality representation in the GHI calculation.
• The GHI measures undernourishment, child stunting, child wasting, and child mortality.
• Despite government welfare schemes, concerns persist over nutritional outcomes, particularly among women and children.
• The debate highlights the complex challenges in accurately assessing and addressing hunger and malnutrition in large, diverse nations.

Evaluate the socio-economic implications of high rates of child stunting, wasting, and undernourishment on India's human capital and development.

articles.headline: India Seeks Further Review of Indus Water Treaty Provisions Amid Pakistan's Concerns
articles.explainer:
• India has initiated a request for further review of certain provisions of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT) with Pakistan, citing evolving hydrological conditions.
• This comes amidst Pakistan's persistent concerns over India's hydroelectric projects on the Western rivers.
• India maintains its projects are fully compliant with the treaty's provisions for 'run-of-the-river' schemes.
• The IWT, brokered by the World Bank, has largely withstood geopolitical tensions for decades.
• Discussions aim to clarify interpretations and address modern-day water management challenges in the basin.

highlights: Supreme Court expands 'Polluter Pays' principle, mandating advanced environmental restoration funds from industries.
India's Q2 FY26 GDP growth projected to remain robust, driven by manufacturing and services sectors.
ISRO successfully completes orbital maneuver for Venus Orbiter Mission 'Shukrayaan-1', marking a major space milestone.
Union Cabinet approves 'National Urban Green Mobility Policy 2025' to promote sustainable urban transport and EVs.
India calls for urgent and comprehensive UN Security Council reforms at the UN General Assembly session.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the recent Supreme Court ruling on the 'Polluter Pays' principle, consider the following statements:
1. The ruling mandates industrial units in all zones to contribute to a central environmental restoration corpus.
2. The 'Polluter Pays' principle was first recognized in India in the Vellore Citizens' Welfare Forum v. Union of India case.
3. Article 48A of the Indian Constitution directs the state to protect and improve the environment.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. The ruling directs industrial units specifically in 'ecologically sensitive zones' to contribute, not all zones. Statement 2 is correct. The 'Polluter Pays' Principle was first recognized in India in the Vellore Citizens' Welfare Forum v. Union of India case (1996). Statement 3 is correct. Article 48A (Directive Principle of State Policy) states that the State shall endeavour to protect and improve the environment and to safeguard the forests and wild life of the country.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following statements best describes the primary objective of the 'Bharat-e-Mandi' platform recently launched by the Union Agriculture Ministry?
options: (a) To provide direct financial subsidies to small and marginal farmers.
(b) To connect Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs) directly with buyers, reducing intermediaries.
(c) To regulate the prices of essential agricultural commodities in retail markets.
(d) To facilitate international export of organic agricultural produce only.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) To connect Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs) directly with buyers, reducing intermediaries.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The 'Bharat-e-Mandi' platform is specifically designed to empower Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs) by providing them with a direct e-commerce platform to sell their produce to buyers, thereby reducing the role of intermediaries and ensuring better price realization for farmers. Options (a), (c), and (d) are not the primary stated objectives of this specific platform.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding India's 'Shukrayaan-1' Venus Orbiter Mission:
1. It is designed to study Venus's atmospheric composition and surface features.
2. The mission carries a high-resolution Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) as one of its key payloads.
3. It is ISRO's first interplanetary mission to a planet other than Mars.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct, as Shukrayaan-1 aims to study Venus's atmospheric composition, surface features, and subsurface structure. Statement 2 is correct, a high-resolution SAR is indeed a key payload. Statement 3 is incorrect. ISRO's Mars Orbiter Mission (Mangalyaan) was its first interplanetary mission. Shukrayaan-1 would be its second interplanetary mission to a planet, and first to Venus.

prelimsMocks.question:
With respect to the 'National Urban Green Mobility Policy 2025', which of the following are its key focus areas?
1. Promoting electric vehicles (EVs).
2. Enhancing public transport infrastructure.
3. Developing city-specific Green Mobility Plans (GMPs).
4. Mandating the use of fossil fuels for commercial transport.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statements 1, 2, and 3 are correct. The policy focuses on promoting EVs, public transport, non-motorized transport, and mandates city-specific Green Mobility Plans. Statement 4 is incorrect; the policy aims to reduce carbon emissions and promote green mobility, which is contrary to mandating the use of fossil fuels.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT an indicator used to calculate the Global Hunger Index (GHI)?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Global Hunger Index (GHI) uses four indicators to calculate its scores: undernourishment, child stunting (low height for age), child wasting (low weight for height), and child mortality (death before the age of five). Adult obesity is not one of the indicators for the GHI.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's expanded 'Polluter Pays' principle signifies a shift towards proactive environmental governance. Discuss the implications of this ruling for industrial development, environmental justice, and the future of sustainable development in India. (250 words)

Discuss implications for industry (compliance costs, R&D for cleaner tech), environmental justice (accountability, community benefit), and sustainable development (balancing growth with ecology). GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Discovers Ancient Trade Route Artifacts in Ladakh
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has unearthed a significant collection of artifacts along what is believed to be an ancient trade route in Ladakh.
• Discoveries include pottery shards, ancient coins, tools, and remnants of temporary settlements, dating back to the Kushan period.
• The findings provide crucial insights into historical trade networks connecting India with Central Asia and Tibet.
• Highlights Ladakh's strategic importance as a cultural and economic crossroads in ancient times.
• Further excavation is underway to map the full extent of the trade route and its associated sites.

Discuss the role of geography in shaping ancient trade routes and cultural exchanges across the Himalayan region.
Evaluate the contribution of archaeological findings to reconstructing India's ancient history and its interactions with neighboring civilizations.

GS2.headline: India Hosts 15th 'Delhi Dialogue' Focusing on Strengthening ASEAN-India Strategic Partnership
GS2.explainer:
• The 15th edition of the annual Delhi Dialogue is underway, bringing together policymakers, academics, and business leaders from India and ASEAN nations.
• The theme for this year emphasizes enhancing economic, strategic, and cultural cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region.
• Discussions focus on maritime security, digital economy integration, sustainable development, and regional connectivity.
• India reiterates its 'Act East Policy' commitment and its central role in fostering regional stability and prosperity.
• The dialogue aims to identify new avenues for collaboration and address common challenges.

• Universities and colleges are introducing skill-based courses, apprenticeships, and industry collaborations to enhance employability.

GS2.headline: India Calls for Urgent UN Security Council Reforms at UNGA Session
GS2.explainer:
• India's representative at the United Nations General Assembly (UNGA) reiterated the urgent need for comprehensive reforms of the UN Security Council (UNSC).
• Emphasized that the current structure of the UNSC does not reflect contemporary global realities and power dynamics.
• Called for an expansion of both permanent and non-permanent seats, with India advocating for its rightful place as a permanent member.
• Highlighted the necessity for a more representative, transparent, and effective global governance body.
• The demand aligns with the long-standing G4 group's push for UNSC expansion.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
prelimsPoints: UNSC has 5 permanent members (P5): USA, UK, France, Russia, China.
UNSC has 10 non-permanent members elected for two-year terms.
India is a part of the G4 group (India, Brazil, Germany, Japan) advocating for permanent UNSC seats.
UNGA is the main deliberative, policymaking, and representative organ of the UN.
The need for UNSC reform is based on changed geopolitical realities since 1945.

Discuss the structural and political challenges hindering UNSC reforms, including resistance from existing P5 members.
Evaluate the implications of an unreformed UNSC for global peace, security, and the legitimacy of multilateralism.

Suggest a roadmap for India and like-minded nations to build consensus and advance the agenda for comprehensive UN reforms.
GS2.imageDescription: A graphic depicting the UN Security Council chamber with India's flag prominently displayed among other nations, symbolizing its aspiration for a permanent seat.
GS2.trivia: The last major reform of the UNSC occurred in 1965, when the number of non-permanent members was increased from six to ten.

GS2.headline: Centre-State Financial Relations: 16th Finance Commission Begins Consultations with States
GS2.explainer:
• The 16th Finance Commission has commenced its consultative meetings with state governments and other stakeholders.
• The commission's primary mandate is to recommend the vertical and horizontal devolution of taxes between the Centre and states.
• Discussions focus on states' fiscal positions, revenue mobilization challenges, and expenditure requirements.
• Also tasked with reviewing disaster relief funds and suggesting measures for fiscal consolidation.
• Recommendations are crucial for shaping India's federal fiscal architecture for the next five years.

GS2.headline: Ministry of Jal Shakti Launches 'Jal Jeevan Mission 2.0' for Sustainable Water Management

• The new phase emphasizes source sustainability, water quality monitoring, greywater management, and water conservation at the community level.
• Aims to ensure 'Har Ghar Jal' (tap water to every rural household) with a greater thrust on long-term operational and maintenance mechanisms.
• Integrates advanced technology for real-time monitoring and citizen engagement for water governance.
• Addresses challenges like water scarcity, contamination, and equitable distribution in rural areas.

GS3.headline: Supreme Court Expands Scope of 'Polluter Pays' Principle, Mandates Advanced Environmental Restoration Funds
GS3.explainer:
• The Apex Court upholds and significantly expands the 'Polluter Pays' principle, emphasizing proactive measures.
• Directs industrial units in ecologically sensitive zones to contribute to a central environmental restoration corpus.
• Mandates a national framework for robust environmental damage assessment, compensation, and restoration disbursement.
• The ruling aims to shift focus from punitive actions to comprehensive, long-term ecological sustainability.
• Impacts industries, environmental governance, and future infrastructure projects across the country.

GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of a green landscape with an industrial complex in the background, symbolizing environmental impact and restoration efforts.
GS3.trivia: The 'Polluter Pays' Principle was incorporated into Principle 16 of the Rio Declaration on Environment and Development (1992).

GS3.headline: ISRO Announces Successful Orbital Maneuver for Venus Orbiter Mission 'Shukrayaan-1'
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) confirms the successful completion of a crucial orbital maneuver for its Venus Orbiter Mission, Shukrayaan-1.
• This maneuver propels the spacecraft into its final scientific observation orbit around Venus.
• Shukrayaan-1 is designed to study Venus's atmospheric composition, surface features, and subsurface structure.
• The mission carries several indigenous scientific payloads, including a high-resolution synthetic aperture radar (SAR).
• This marks a significant milestone in India's interplanetary exploration capabilities.

India's past interplanetary missions: Chandrayaan series (Moon), Mars Orbiter Mission (Mangalyaan).

GS3.trivia: Venus is often called Earth's 'sister planet' due to similar size and mass, but has a vastly different, extremely hot and toxic atmosphere.

GS3.headline: Union Cabinet Approves 'National Urban Green Mobility Policy 2025'
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet has given its nod to the 'National Urban Green Mobility Policy 2025', aimed at fostering sustainable urban transport.
• The policy focuses on promoting electric vehicles (EVs), public transport, non-motorized transport, and smart mobility solutions.
• Key objectives include reducing carbon emissions, improving air quality, and enhancing urban liveability.
• It mandates states and urban local bodies to develop city-specific Green Mobility Plans (GMPs) with central support.
• The policy integrates technological innovations and financial incentives for adoption of green mobility.

GS3.headline: India-Japan Joint Military Exercise 'Dharma Guardian' Concludes, Focus on Counter-Terrorism

GS3.headline: New Report Highlights Increasing Frequency of Extreme Weather Events in Indian Subcontinent
GS3.explainer:

• The report details increased frequency and intensity of heatwaves, droughts, floods, and cyclones across the Indian subcontinent.
• Attributes the trend primarily to anthropogenic climate change and regional atmospheric-oceanic interactions.
• Calls for urgent adaptation strategies, improved early warning systems, and climate-resilient infrastructure.
• Emphasizes the disproportionate impact on vulnerable communities and agricultural sectors.

GS3.headline: Government Launches 'Bharat-e-Mandi' Platform to Boost Agricultural Trade for FPOs
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Agriculture Ministry has launched 'Bharat-e-Mandi', a national e-commerce platform specifically for agricultural produce.
• The platform aims to connect Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs) directly with buyers, reducing intermediaries and ensuring better prices for farmers.
• It integrates warehousing, logistics, and quality assurance services to streamline the agricultural supply chain.
• The initiative is part of the government's broader vision to double farmers' income and empower FPOs.
• Offers a transparent and efficient marketplace, leveraging digital technology for agricultural trade.

GS3.headline: Breakthrough in Indigenous Development of Next-Gen Quantum Computing Components
GS3.explainer:
• Indian scientists at the National Quantum Mission (NQM) have announced a significant breakthrough in developing indigenous components for quantum computers.

• This development places India among a select few nations working on full-stack quantum technology.
• Aims to reduce reliance on foreign technology and accelerate India's quantum computing capabilities for strategic applications.
• The NQM is a government initiative to foster research and development in quantum technologies.
